executive_brief:
The threat landscape has fundamentally shifted this week. The traditional 'patch window' has collapsed. We are witnessing the dawn of AI-driven vulnerability discovery, where tools like Anthropic's Mythos are identifying and weaponizing zero-days at a pace that outstrips human remediation cycles [64, 77, 321]. Simultaneously, ransomware groups like Storm-1175 are deploying Medusa ransomware within 24 hours of vulnerability disclosure, effectively rendering standard patch management strategies obsolete [1, 17]. This is not a gradual evolution; it is an acceleration of attack velocity that demands immediate architectural changes, not just faster patching.

Beyond the digital realm, the physical world is under direct assault. Iran-linked actors are actively compromising Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) at US critical infrastructure sites, causing real-world operational disruptions [74, 86]. This convergence of AI-accelerated cyberattacks and state-sponsored physical sabotage creates a dual-threat environment where availability and safety are at stake. Organizations relying on perimeter defenses or standard vulnerability management will find themselves defenseless against this new velocity.

threat_stories.headline: The Mythos Effect: AI Collapses the Zero-Day Timeline
threat_stories.narrative:
Anthropic's 'Mythos' AI model has demonstrated the ability to autonomously discover and exploit thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers [64, 77]. This capability, showcased under Project Glasswing, represents a structural shift in cybersecurity. Unlike human researchers who require weeks or months to analyze code, Mythos identifies flaws and generates working exploits in minutes [321]. The implication is severe: the window between vulnerability discovery and weaponization has shrunk from months to hours. 

This is not theoretical. The AI model has already identified flaws in critical infrastructure software, including a 27-year-old bug in OpenBSD [64]. While the tool is currently restricted to select partners, the technology proves that automated, AI-driven vulnerability discovery is now a reality. Attackers do not need to wait for vendor disclosures; they can use similar agentic AI to find their own zero-days. Defenders must assume that any unpatched vulnerability is already known to an adversary capable of AI-assisted exploitation.

timeline.event: Anthropic announces Project Glasswing and Claude Mythos capabilities
timeline.significance: Public confirmation of AI-driven zero-day discovery at scale

timeline.event: Reports confirm Mythos found thousands of flaws in major OS and browsers
timeline.significance: Validation of the scale of the threat to existing software stacks

timeline.event: CSA urges CISOs to prepare for 'Mythos-Ready' security
timeline.significance: Industry acknowledgment that current security models are insufficient

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. All organizations running unpatched software or relying on signature-based detection are at immediate risk. The 'unknown unknown' category of vulnerabilities is shrinking rapidly.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement AI-driven defensive tools that can detect anomalous behavior rather than known signatures. Prioritize patching based on exploitability probability, not just CVSS scores. Assume zero-days exist in your environment and isolate critical assets.

threat_stories.headline: Storm-1175: The 24-Hour Ransomware Window
threat_stories.narrative:
The Storm-1175 group, linked to China, has weaponized the speed of modern vulnerability disclosure. They are deploying Medusa ransomware within 24 hours of a flaw being disclosed [1, 17]. This tactic bypasses the traditional 'patch window' where defenders have days or weeks to remediate. Storm-1175 chains multiple vulnerabilities, including zero-days and N-days, to breach networks, exfiltrate data, and encrypt systems in a single day [19, 20].

This operational tempo indicates a high level of sophistication and automated tooling. They target healthcare, education, and enterprise sectors in the UK, US, and Australia [1]. The group leverages both fresh bugs and known flaws, suggesting they have a pipeline of exploits ready to deploy the moment a patch is announced. For defenders, this means the moment a vulnerability is public, it is effectively weaponized against them.

timeline.event: Microsoft warns Storm-1175 is speeding ransomware attacks
timeline.significance: Official confirmation of the 24-hour deployment cycle

timeline.event: Storm-1175 deploys Medusa within 24 hours of flaw disclosure
timeline.significance: Demonstration of the tactic in the wild

timeline.event: Microsoft identifies Storm-1175 exploiting over 16 vulnerabilities since 2023
timeline.significance: Evidence of a sustained, long-term campaign

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Healthcare and education sectors are primary targets. Organizations with slow patching cycles are guaranteed to be compromised.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement network segmentation to limit lateral movement. Deploy real-time isolation capabilities for affected systems. Assume internet-facing assets are already compromised and monitor for Medusa indicators.

threat_stories.headline: Iran's PLC Campaign: Physical Disruption of Critical Infrastructure
threat_stories.narrative:
Iran-linked APT actors, including CyberAv3ngers, are actively targeting internet-exposed Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) at US critical infrastructure facilities [74, 86]. Unlike traditional cyber espionage, these attacks cause real-world operational disruptions. Attackers gain access to Rockwell Automation and Siemens PLCs, extract project files, and alter SCADA and HMI display data [86].

Censys identified over 5,000 internet-exposed Rockwell Automation PLCs globally, primarily in the US, many running outdated firmware [256]. The attackers use leased overseas infrastructure to connect to these devices via ports like 44818 and 502 [74]. The goal is not just data theft but the ability to manipulate physical processes, posing a direct threat to energy, water, and manufacturing sectors. This campaign highlights the vulnerability of Operational Technology (OT) connected to public networks.

timeline.event: FBI/CISA/NSA warn of Iran-linked actors targeting OT
timeline.significance: Joint advisory confirming active threat to critical infrastructure

timeline.event: Iran-linked PLC attacks cause real-world disruption at US infra sites
Censys finds 5,219 devices exposed to attacks by Iranian APTs
timeline.significance: Quantification of the exposed attack surface

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Energy, water, and manufacturing sectors face immediate risk of physical disruption. Internet-exposed OT devices are high-value targets.
threat_stories.action_required: Disconnect PLCs from the internet where possible. Implement network segmentation to isolate OT environments. Monitor OT ports (44818, 502) for suspicious traffic from overseas IPs.

threat_stories.headline: The Death of the Patch Window: Marimo and Flowise
threat_stories.narrative:
The vulnerability management lifecycle is breaking down. The Marimo Python notebook platform suffered a critical RCE flaw (CVE-2026-39987) that was exploited in the wild within 10 hours of public disclosure [210, 244]. Similarly, Flowise AI Agent Builder (CVE-2025-59528) is under active exploitation, with 12,000+ instances exposed [16].

These incidents demonstrate that attackers have automated scanning and exploitation tools ready to pounce on new vulnerabilities immediately. The Marimo flaw allowed unauthenticated attackers to gain full system control via a WebSocket endpoint [294]. Flowise's flaw allowed arbitrary code execution through the CustomMCP node [61]. This trend indicates that 'patch Tuesday' is no longer a safe harbor; if a vulnerability is disclosed, it is weaponized before most organizations can even read the advisory.

timeline.event: Flowise AI Agent Builder under active CVSS 10.0 RCE exploitation
timeline.significance: Confirmation of widespread exploitation of AI tools

timeline.event: Marimo RCE flaw exploited within 10 hours of disclosure
timeline.significance: Demonstration of the collapsed patch window

timeline.event: Critical Marimo flaw now under active exploitation
timeline.significance: Ongoing threat to data science environments

threat_stories.impact_assessment: High. Data science, AI development, and open-source tool users are at risk. Internet-facing instances of these tools are likely compromised.
threat_stories.action_required: Update Marimo to version 0.23.0 or later immediately. Isolate Flowise instances from public networks. Disable the CustomMCP node if not required.

threat_stories.headline: Adobe Reader Zero-Day: Persistent Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative:
A zero-day vulnerability in Adobe Reader (CVE-2026-34621) has been actively exploited for months, with malicious PDFs used to fingerprint targets and exfiltrate data [134, 140]. The exploit leverages the JavaScript engine to execute privileged APIs, potentially allowing sandbox escape [174]. Adobe has released emergency patches, but the campaign has been ongoing since at least December 2025 [149].

The attack vector is simple: a malicious PDF file. Attackers use this to steal data without user interaction beyond opening the file [176]. This persistent exploitation highlights the risk of widely used software with large attack surfaces. Even with patches available, the initial infection rate is high due to the ubiquity of PDF readers in enterprise environments.

timeline.event: Exploitation of Adobe Reader zero-day begins
Researchers confirm active exploitation of unpatched Adobe Reader
Adobe rolls out emergency fix for Acrobat Reader zero-day
timeline.significance: Vendor response to the active threat

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. All users of Adobe Reader are at risk. The exploit is sophisticated and has been active for months.
threat_stories.action_required: Install the emergency update for Adobe Acrobat and Reader immediately. Disable JavaScript in Adobe Reader settings. Avoid opening PDF files from untrusted sources.

actor_profiles.name: Storm-1175
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deploying Medusa ransomware within 24 hours of vulnerability disclosure. Chaining zero-days and N-days for rapid compromise.

actor_profiles.name: CyberAv3ngers
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Targeting internet-exposed PLCs (Rockwell, Siemens) to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.

actor_profiles.name: APT28 (Fancy Bear)
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Hijacking SOHO routers (MikroTik, TP-Link) for DNS hijacking and AitM attacks. Deploying PRISMEX malware.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Breach of Rockstar Games via Snowflake integrator. Targeting high-value corporations for data theft.

actor_profiles.name: Lazarus Group
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Supply chain compromises (Axios/OpenAI). Registering real US LLCs to spread malware (GraphAlgo).

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Zero-day vulnerability allowing remote code execution via malicious PDF. Exploited for months to fingerprint targets and exfiltrate data.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Install emergency update released by Adobe. Disable JavaScript in Reader settings.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated RCE via WebSocket endpoint allowing full system control. Exploited within 10 hours of disclosure.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to Marimo version 0.23.0 or later.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Code injection in CustomMCP node allowing remote code execution. 12,000+ instances exposed.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Flowise to the latest patched version. Disable CustomMCP node.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper access control allowing unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication via API and escalate privileges.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Install hotfix for EMS 7.4.5/7.4.6. Upgrade to 7.4.7.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Code injection vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. Added to CISA KEV catalog.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest patch released by Ivanti immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unrestricted file upload allowing remote code execution. Affects versions before 2.8.7.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to version 2.8.7 or later immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authorization bypass allowing attackers to gain root-level access to host systems via oversized HTTP requests.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Docker Engine to version 29.3.1 or later.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: RTF file exploitation bypassing security controls to force WebDAV connection. Used by APT28.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply security patches released after January 26, 2026. Disable RTF preview.

hunting_leads.title: Marimo WebSocket Exploitation
hunting_leads.context: Marimo instances are being targeted for unauthenticated RCE via the /terminal/ws endpoint.
hunting_leads.query: index=web_access src_ip!=internal dest_port=8787 path="/terminal/ws" | stats count by src_ip

hunting_leads.title: Storm-1175 Medusa Indicators
hunting_leads.context: Storm-1175 deploys Medusa ransomware rapidly after initial access. Look for Medusa-specific artifacts.

hunting_leads.title: Iran PLC Traffic Anomalies
hunting_leads.context: Iran-linked actors target PLCs via ports 44818, 502, 2222. Look for connections from overseas IPs.
hunting_leads.query: dest_port IN (44818, 502, 2222) AND src_country NOT IN (US, CA, UK)

hunting_leads.title: Flowise RCE Signs
hunting_leads.context: Flowise CustomMCP node is being exploited for RCE. Monitor for suspicious node configurations.

hunting_leads.title: Adobe Reader JS Execution
hunting_leads.context: Adobe Reader zero-day uses JavaScript to exfiltrate data. Monitor for suspicious JS execution in Reader.

statistics.key_changes: The 'patch window' has collapsed to hours (Marimo, Storm-1175). AI-driven vulnerability discovery (Mythos) is now a confirmed reality.
emerging_trends: Ransomware deployment within 24 hours of disclosure
declining_threats: Traditional spearphishing (being replaced by automated exploitation)
Long-term dwell time (attacks are now rapid)
